recalls.Description: Luyuan is recalling the X-Pro Eagle 40cc Youth ATV and the FRP Sahara 40cc Youth ATV due to serious safety concerns. These models, identified by their "X PRO" and "FRP" labels, come in various colors and have been found to violate mandatory safety standards for all-terrain vehicles. The mechanical suspension of these ATVs does not meet safety requirements, increasing the risk of crashes. Additionally, the handlebars can cause laceration injuries if a child rider impacts them at high speeds.
recalls.Title: Luyuan Recalls Youth ATVs Due to Crash Hazard Risk and Safety Standard Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should cease using these ATVs immediately and reach out to Luyuan at 855-663-2121 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. recalls.Description:
Polaris Industries is recalling specific VINs of the 2025-2026 Ranger XP 1000 NorthStar and Ranger Crew XP 1000 recreational off-road vehicles (ROVs). These vehicles, available in various colors including black, blue, and red, come in three and six-seat configurations and feature the "POLARIS" logo on the front grille. The VIN can be found on the left rear frame under the cargo box on the driver's side.

The recall is prompted by a potential fire hazard caused by an improperly routed alternator cable that may short-circuit against the negative battery post, leading to serious injury risks. Owners should take immediate action to verify if their vehicle is affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls 2025-2026 Ranger XP 1000 NorthStar and Ranger Crew XP 1000 ROVs Due to Fire Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. You can also reach out to Polaris' owner connections team via email at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their online forum at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

recalls.Description: Textron E-Z-GO is recalling specific models of Personal Transportation Vehicles (PTVs) due to potential fuel leaks that could lead to serious injuries or fire hazards. The affected models include the RXV Freedom and Valor PTVs from model years 2020 to 2025, as well as the TXT Freedom and Valor PTVs from model years 2020 to 2022. Serial numbers for the RXV models range from 5538278 to 5920722, while the TXT models have serial numbers from 3455024 to 3582057. These vehicles, often referred to as golf carts, are primarily used for transporting individuals.

recalls.Description: FENGQS is recalling its F7 Pro electric bikes due to potential fire and burn hazards associated with the lithium-ion batteries. These bikes, sold in blue, were manufactured on April 21, 2024, and feature the brand name "FENGQS" on the downtube and the model number "F7 Pro" on the chain stay near the rear wheel. The battery's specifications, including a capacity of 21.5Ah and the production date, are labeled on the front of the battery.
recalls.Title: FENGQS F7 Pro Electric Bikes Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact FENGQS at 607-307-2127 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@fengqs.work, or visit www.fengqs.com/pages/recalls. You can also access the recall information by navigating to the "Recalls" section on their website.

Products.Name: FENGQS F7 Pro Electric Bike
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 100

Injuries.Name: FENGQS has received 13 reports of e-bikes igniting, two resulting in fires with property damage totaling $4,000.

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen Fengqisi Car Industry Co., Ltd., dba FENGQS, of China

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from May 2024 to December 2024, priced between $700 and $1,200.

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and ignition from the lithium-ion battery, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the e-bikes immediately and contact FENGQS for a full refund. Write your name, the word "recalled," and the current date next to the battery's production date with a permanent marker. Submit a photo showing this information to recall@fengqs.work and confirm proper disposal of the battery according to local regulations. Do not dispose of the battery in regular trash or recycling bins. Check with your local hazardous waste collection center for proper disposal options.

recalls.metaDescription: FENGQS recalls F7 Pro electric bikes due to fire hazards. Contact for refund and safe disposal instructions.

recalls.Description: 509 has issued a recall for several models of its Tactical helmets, including the Tactical 3.0 (Youth, Mountain, and Trail) and Tactical 2.0 (Youth and Mountain) helmets, all manufactured in 2023. These helmets bear the 509 logo and are designed for safety, but they may not provide adequate protection in a crash, leading to potential head injury risks. recalls.Description: Yamaha has broadened its recall to encompass additional golf car vehicles and a more thorough repair solution. This recall now includes approximately 352,000 units, expanding on previous recalls affecting 11,000 cars in July 2023, 5,000 in February 2022, 145,000 in October 2018, and 161,000 in February 2018. The affected models include various Yamaha Golf Cars, Personal Transportation Vehicles, and UMAX models from multiple production years, specifically from 2016 to 2024. The recall addresses a serious safety concern where the accelerator pedal spring may not return to idle, leading to potential crash hazards. Consumers are urged to check their vehicle's serial number for inclusion in this recall and to seek assistance if needed.
recalls.Title: Yamaha Expands Recall of Golf Car Vehicles Due to Crash Risk; New Models and Repair for Accelerator Pedal Spring Assembly
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Yamaha toll-free at 866-747-4027 at any time or visit https://www.yamahamotorsports.com/Content.php?content=cpsc-recalls or https://yamaha-motor.com/ and click on 'CPSC Recalls' at the bottom of the page for more details.

CFMOTO is recalling its 2024 CFORCE 800 Touring and 2024 CFORCE 1000 Touring all-terrain vehicles (ATVs) due to a serious safety concern. These recreational vehicles are available in various colors including gray, orange, black, and camouflage. The CFMOTO logo is located on the rear fender sides, while the CFORCE branding is found on the upper side panels. Each vehicle's unique Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) can be found stamped on the frame behind the right rear wheel.

The issue arises from a potential failure of the throttle lever to return to the idle position when released, which could lead to unexpected acceleration and increase the risk of a crash. Consumers are urged to cease using these ATVs immediately and reach out to an authorized CFMOTO dealer for a complimentary repair. Textron Specialized Vehicles has expanded its recall to include all model years 2022, 2023, and 2024 of the Arctic Cat Prowler Pro and Pro Crew, as well as the Tracker Off Road 800SX and 800SX Crew Side by Side Recreational Off Highway Vehicles. These vehicles, available in various colors, feature a "Prowler Pro" decal on the Arctic Cat models and an "800SX" decal on the Tracker models, located just outside the driver and passenger doors.

The safety concern arises from the vehicles potentially moving when parked on an incline, even when the digital display indicates that the vehicle is in the "park" position. recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. You can also reach Polaris' owner connections team at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their online forum at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

recalls.Description: Polaris is recalling specific VINs of the 2023-2024 Sportsman 850, XP 1000, and XP 1000 S all-terrain vehicles (ATVs). recalls.Description: Textron Specialized Vehicles has issued a recall for the 2024 Arctic Cat Mountain 600 Snowmobiles, available in multiple colors. These snowmobiles feature an 'M' shaped like a mountain on their side panels, and the 10th digit of the VIN is 'R', which can be found on the tunnel section or on a decal at the rear of the tunnel.
recalls.Title: Recall of 2024 Arctic Cat Mountain 600 Snowmobiles Due to Steering Control Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Textron Specialized Vehicles at 800-279-2281, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at arcticcatwarranty@textron.com or visit https://arcticcat.txtsv.com/owners/recalls for more details.

Products.Name: 2024 Arctic Cat Mountain 600 Snowmobiles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 600

Recalled Artic Cat Mountain 600 Snowmobile (white)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received six reports of cross pins that have become loose or have fallen out. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Textron Specialized Vehicles, of Augusta, Georgia

Retailers.Name: Available at Arctic Cat dealers across the country from September 2023 through June 2024, priced between $14,500 and $16,100.

Hazards.Name: Improperly swagged pin on the steering knuckle's u-joint, which may lead to loss of steering control and a potential crash.

recalls.Description: Kawasaki USA has announced a recall for the 2024 Mule PRO-FX™ 1000, PRO-FXR™ 1000, and PRO-FXT™ 1000 off-highway utility vehicles due to a potential fire hazard. These vehicles, available in various colors including Metallic Sierra Blue and Firecracker Red, may experience improper combustion when starting the engine, increasing the risk of fire. recalls.Description:
Polaris is recalling specific MATRYX snowmobiles from model years 2022 to 2024, which are equipped with PATRIOT 650 and 850 engines. These snowmobiles, available in various black and custom color combinations, may pose an injury risk due to a potential failure of the primary (drive) clutch. The recall includes service parts with part numbers 1323613 (e-start) and 1323614 (manual start). The VIN and model number can be found on the right side of the vehicle's frame, while 'POLARIS' is stamped on the front side panel.

The affected models include the MATRYX VOYAGEUR, Indy, Switchback, RMK, and SKS across multiple years. Consumers are urged to stop using these snowmobiles immediately and reach out to Polaris for a free repair to replace the faulty clutch. Polaris is actively notifying registered owners and dealers about this recall.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls MATRYX Snowmobiles with PATRIOT 650 and 850 Engines Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Visit www.polaris.com and click on 'Product Safety News' under the 'Safety' section or go to www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/ to check if your vehicle identification number (VIN) is affected. You can also reach Polaris' owner connections team at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their online forum at www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

recalls.Description:
Polaris Industries has issued a recall for the Model Year 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic recreational off-road vehicles (ROVs). These vehicles, available in camouflage and white, feature a three-seat design and have the 'POLARIS' logo on the front grille. The model number and VIN can be found on the left rear frame, beneath the cargo box on the driver's side.

The recall is prompted by a safety concern where pressing both the brake and throttle pedals at the same time can deactivate the throttle. If the brake is then released, the vehicle may unexpectedly move, increasing the risk of a crash and potential serious injuries.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls Ranger XP Kinetic Off-Road Vehicles Due to Potential Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled vehicles immediately and contact Polaris at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. For more information, visit www.polaris.com and click on 'Product Safety News' or check recalls at www.polaris.com/en-us/recalls. For direct assistance, email Owner.Connections@polaris.com or use the online forum at www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.
